---
title: "Install R and the packages you need"
---

Do this **before day 1** so the first session is spent on the maths, not on setup.

## 1. Install R

Install a recent [R](https://cran.r-project.org/) (4.3 or newer) from CRAN.

## 2. Install an editor

We recommend [RStudio Desktop](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/) (free).
[VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) with the
[R extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=REditorSupport.r)
also works.

## 3. Install the packages used in the course

```r
install.packages(c(
  "tidyverse",   # data wrangling and plotting
  "Matrix"       # dense and sparse matrices
  # ... add packages here as the material is written
))
```

Bioconductor packages (for the single-cell and omics applications) install via
`BiocManager`:

```r
install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install(c(
  # ... add Bioconductor packages here as the material is written
))
```

## 4. Check it works

```r
library(tidyverse)
A <- matrix(c(2, 1, 1, 3), nrow = 2)
b <- c(1, 2)
solve(A, b)   # should print the solution to Ax = b
```

If that prints two numbers without error, you are ready for day 1.
